target.name: Parallel Systems
target.founder: Matt Soule, John Howard, Ben Stabler

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Parallel Systems is a Series A transportation company founded by Matt Soule, John Howard, and Ben Stabler. The company has raised $100M to date. The transportation tech sector contains eight private comparable companies, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Pipedream, and FleetZero; no public peers were identified as sufficiently analogous.

Of two valuation methods examined, only stage-progression proved applicable. The peer-median approach was not executable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within transportation—while three transportation companies exist in the broader database, none met the minimum threshold of three datapoints within defined valuation tiers. The stage-progression method uses total raised ($100M) discounted to a $6M proxy base, then applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implying a $12M valuation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $10M to $14M, with a median point of $12M. This range reflects uncertainty inherent in a single-method triangulation. The key limitation is the absence of granular, same-stage transportation comparables; a fuller peer set would either validate this estimate or reveal sector-specific multiples that could materially shift the range. The $100M capital raised also warrants scrutiny—stage-progression models typically anchor to earlier-stage funding. Clarification on cash deployment and burn trajectory would refine confidence in this valuation.
